description: Mixed loop pattern: per-item fulfillment calls, then one summary notification after all items complete (executeOnce on the rollup step).

conversation.text: Hourly, sync new orders from GET https://api.example.com/orders/new — the API returns a JSON envelope {orders: [...]}, so split that into individual items first. Call POST https://api.example.com/fulfillment once per order. When all orders in that run are processed, send one notification via POST https://api.example.com/notify with the total count and how many succeeded.

outcomeExpectations: Each new order triggers its own fulfillment POST (per-item processing after splitting the orders envelope into individual items).
The final notify POST runs once per workflow execution with rollup totals (total count and success count), not once per order — executeOnce or equivalent on the notify step.

executionScenarios.name: three-orders-one-summary
executionScenarios.description: Three orders fulfilled; one notify POST with counts
executionScenarios.dataSetup: GET https://api.example.com/orders/new returns { "orders": [{ "id": "ORD-1" }, { "id": "ORD-2" }, { "id": "ORD-3" }] }. Each POST https://api.example.com/fulfillment returns { "ok": true, "status": "fulfilled" }. POST https://api.example.com/notify returns { "ok": true }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. Fulfillment POST is called three times (once per order). Notify POST is called exactly once and its body references a total of 3 orders and 3 successes (or equivalent counts). A workflow that POSTs to notify three separate times FAILS.
